description: Evaluates whether the readability-reviewer skill catches lying names and ambiguous parameters without nagging on trivial renames or cosmetic cleanup.

cases.name: lying-name-process-normalizes-emails
cases.notes: Function is exported as `process` but it specifically normalizes emails. The name promises far more than it delivers; the skill should flag the misleading name.

cases.name: ambiguous-boolean-parameter
cases.notes: Callers will write `send(msg, true)` with no hint what true means. Ambiguous boolean parameter; the docstring shifts the burden to the reader. Common readability smell.

cases.name: trivial-rename-only
cases.notes: Rename from `sum` to the more descriptive `sumLineItems`. No behavior change, no new smell introduced. Skill should stay silent.

cases.name: extracted-named-constant
cases.notes: Magic number replaced with a named constant. Strictly a readability improvement; there is nothing to flag.
